zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Scales II於1177年出生在英國坎布利其爾時，他的父親William de Scales II30歲，母親Mrs William De Scales28歲。他於大約1220年與Alice of Caxton在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾卡可頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1231年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Scales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: plural form of   Scale  , naming a herdsman living and working on a summer pasture, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Scales, mostly minor places in Lancashire and Yorkshire.   Scholes   is a dialect variant of the same name.

(of Norman origin): habitational name either from Écalles-Alix (Seine-Maritime), Écalles (in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime), or from Escalles (Pas-de-Calais). The earliest spellings of these names suggest a derivation from Latin scala, scalae (plural) ‘ladder, staircase, stile’, perhaps applied to a steep slope or path, although Old Norse skáli (‘herdsman's hut, shieling’, see Scale ) has also been suggested for Écalles. Challis may be a variant of the same name. The surname has also been established in southern Ireland (Limerick) since the 14th century.

variants.name: Scholes
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the places called Scholes in Lancashire and Yorkshire, or from Scole in Norfolk, all named with Middle English scole or scoles ‘herdsman's hut(s)’ (Old Norse skáli). In northern England the name was sometimes interchangeable with Scales , a dialect variant of the same word.

variants.name: Scale
meanings: English: from northern Middle English scale ‘herdsman's hut, shed, shieling’ (Old Norse skáli). The surname may have denoted a herdsman who lived and worked on a summer pasture or someone from any of several mostly minor places so named in Lancashire and Yorkshire. The name often varies between singular and plural forms; see Scales .

variants.name: Schooley
meanings: English (Sussex): variant of Scholey, a habitational name either from Scholey's Bridge in Hemsworth (Yorkshire) or the lost Scholey in Rastrick (Yorkshire), recorded in that spelling in 1399. The placenames derive from Old Norse skáli ‘(temporary) hut, shed, shieling’ + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scales .

variants.name: Schoolcraft
meanings: English: variant of Scowcroft, from Middle English scale, sc(h)ole ‘hut, shed’ (Old Norse skáli) + croft ‘small enclosed field’. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a field containing a hut, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Scowcroft in Oldham (Lancashire) and Scholecroft in Morley (Yorkshire). Compare Scales and Schofield .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Scales II was born in 1177, in Cambridgeshire, England, his father, William de Scales II, was 30 and his mother, Mrs William De Scales, was 28. He married Alice of Caxton about 1220, in Caxton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1231, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Scales II est né en 1177, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Scales II, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs William De Scales, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Alice of Caxton environ 1220, à Caxton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1231, à Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Scales II 1177 in Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Scales II 30 und seine Mutter Mrs William De Scales 28. Er hat um 1220 in Caxton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom Alice of Caxton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1231 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Scales II è nato il nel 1177 nel Cambridgeshire, England, il suo padre, William de Scales II, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mrs William De Scales, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Alice of Caxton circa 1220, nel Caxton, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1231, nel Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Scales II(이)가 1177년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Scales II(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs William De Scales(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 카스턴에서 Alice of Caxton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1231년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Scales II родился в 1177 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Scales II было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs William De Scales, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice of Caxton около 1220 г. в местности какстон, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1231 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет.
